summary: MinIO was eliminated from merged ops/docs product sources and from the noetl cleanup PR branch, SeaweedFS was added as the default object-store backend, RustFS was added as an opt-in backend behind the same canonical object-store Service, and local kind validated both backends plus NoETL worker S3-compatible access. Ops and docs PRs merged. The noetl naming/comment cleanup PR is CI-green but review-blocked, so noetl pointer was not bumped; current ai-meta repos/noetl still points at main and therefore still contains the old wording until #430 merges.

ops: Added raw ci/manifests/seaweedfs and ci/manifests/rustfs manifests. Both expose Service object-store.object-store.svc:9000.
Added Helm values objectStore.kind with SeaweedFS default and RustFS opt-in templates.
Pointed NoETL server/worker configmaps at NOETL_S3_ENDPOINT=http://object-store.object-store.svc.cluster.local:9000 with bucket noetl and noetl-access/noetl-secret credentials.
Updated kind port/cache paths from MinIO-specific storage to object-store-data.
noetl: Kept StoreTier.S3 and endpoint-agnostic S3Backend behavior unchanged.
Updated comments, docstrings, schema descriptions, and static manifests to say S3-compatible storage rather than a backend-specific name.
docs: Swept storage reference pages to use S3-compatible object-store terminology and StoreTier.S3 examples.

static: On the PR branches, rg -n -i "minio|min.io|NOETL_MINIO|MINIO_" repos/ops repos/noetl repos/docs --glob '!**/node_modules/**' --glob '!**/.docusaurus/**' --glob '!**/build/**' returned no product-source hits.
After returning repos/noetl to main because #430 is unmerged, the same grep still reports expected noetl main hits; those are the review-blocked #430 cleanup.
helm template noetl automation/helm/noetl --set objectStore.kind=seaweedfs | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f - passed.
helm template noetl automation/helm/noetl --set objectStore.kind=rustfs | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f - passed.
repos/noetl uv run pytest tests/unit/storage/test_disk_cache_backend.py tests/core/test_result_store_cache_tracking.py tests/core/test_result_store_preview.py passed: 19 passed.
local_kind: SeaweedFS deployed to kind-noetl in namespace object-store and rolled out.
SeaweedFS S3 API smoke through svc/object-store: create bucket noetl, upload smoke/noetl-object-store-smoke.txt, download same object.
SeaweedFS object persisted across object-store pod deletion and restart.
RustFS initially crash-looped due UID 10001 permissions on /logs and hostPath-backed /data. Fixed by adding /logs emptyDir and a root initContainer that chowns/chmods /data and /logs.
RustFS deployed to kind-noetl and rolled out after the permission fix.
RustFS S3 API smoke through svc/object-store: create bucket noetl-rustfs-smoke, upload smoke.txt, download same object.
Local kind was returned to default SeaweedFS after RustFS smoke.
NoETL server/worker configmaps were updated in kind and deployments restarted.
Worker env verified: NOETL_S3_ENDPOINT=http://object-store.object-store.svc.cluster.local:9000, NOETL_STORAGE_CLOUD_TIER=s3, bucket noetl.
In-cluster worker boto3 smoke put/get succeeded via the new object-store service.
After deleting all worker pods and waiting for rollout, the restarted worker read the same object back from object storage.
not_run: Full NoETL large-payload execution with automatic DISK async spill was not run; the durable-tier smoke used direct boto3 from the worker image.
GKE replacement was not applied because ops/docs merged but noetl #430 is still review-blocked, and GKE should consume merged/pointer-aligned state.
known_preexisting_test_limits: A broad repos/noetl pytest selection through system Python failed due missing dependencies.
The uv retry resolved dependencies but broad collection still hit unrelated legacy collection errors for missing/renamed modules and a missing fixture file. Targeted storage tests passed.

next_steps: Review/merge noetl/noetl#430, then bump the repos/noetl pointer in ai-meta.
After all three pointers are merged/aligned, run the GKE smart-adapt phase: distinguish remote S3 bucket from in-cluster object-store, then replace only in-cluster MinIO if present.
Run a true NoETL large-payload execution that spills through ResultHandler/DISK async cloud spill, kill worker pods, and verify report/status rehydrates from object storage.
